growth_records.strain: natural population (Eastern Mediterranean Sea, deep chlorophyll maximum, 115-125 m; mixed HL/LL ecotypes)
growth_records.medium: in situ ultra-oligotrophic Eastern Mediterranean seawater (deep chlorophyll maximum)
growth_records.cite: Wu et al. Single-cell measurements and modelling reveal substantial organic carbon acquisition by Prochlorococcus. Nature Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41564-022-01250-5. [PMC9712107]
growth_records.snippet: the observed C-specific C uptake rate (muC) was only ~0.024 day-1 ... while the observed N-specific N uptake rate (muN) was ~0.16 day-1, indicating a doubling time of ~6 days. ... Prochlorococcus cells at depths of 100-150 m replicate every
growth_records.notes: Wild Prochlorococcus DCM population; growth (division) rate inferred from N-specific N uptake muN ~0.16 day-1 -> doubling ~6 days (stated). Converted to per-hour for growth_rate_per_h: 0.16 day-1 / 24 = 0.0067 h-1; doubling_time_h = 6 days x 24 = 144 h. Photosynthetic C-specific uptake muC ~0.024 day-1 (biomass-C-specific; = 0.001 h-1). muC/muN ~0.15 in field vs ~0.75 in lab culture and ~1 in surface -> authors infer >80% of C for growth is heterotrophic (organic C). Literature cell-cycle growth 0.14-0.25 day-1 at 100-150 m. Natural seawater medium, composition not stated -> medium_composition_stated:false. Rates are specific (day-1, biomass-normalized), not mmol/gDW/h; no exchange fluxes in mmol/gDW/h derivable without cell C quota.

growth_records.cite: Becker et al. Co-culture and biogeography of Prochlorococcus and SAR11. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0365-4. [PMC6775983]
growth_records.snippet: The presence of Marinobacter MIT1353 did, however, increase the growth rate of Prochlorococcus MIT1314 slightly (from 0.64 d−1 to 0.68 d−1)
growth_records.notes: Reported growth rate of Prochlorococcus MIT1314 grown alone = 0.64 d-1. Per-hour: 0.64/24 = 0.02667 h-1. td = 24*ln2/0.64 = 0.693/0.02667 = 26.0 h. Pro99 numeric composition not given (recipe cited to reference); natural Sargasso seawater base. No fluxes reported.

growth_records.strain: MIT1314 (HLII), co-cultured with Marinobacter sp. MIT1353
growth_records.cite: Becker et al. Co-culture and biogeography of Prochlorococcus and SAR11. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0365-4. [PMC6775983]
growth_records.snippet: The presence of Marinobacter MIT1353 did, however, increase the growth rate of Prochlorococcus MIT1314 slightly (from 0.64 d−1 to 0.68 d−1)
growth_records.notes: Reported growth rate of Prochlorococcus MIT1314 in co-culture with Marinobacter MIT1353 = 0.68 d-1. Per-hour: 0.68/24 = 0.02833 h-1. td = 24*ln2/0.68 = 0.693/0.02833 = 24.5 h. No fluxes reported.
